title: Fragrant Sonoran Avocado Stew
description: Malty, crumbly, and reminiscent of traditional Sonoran Mexican cooking, this stew showcases the rich flavors of avocado and tender beef. Serve warm in a cazuela for a hearty and comforting meal.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven or cazuela, brown the short ribs over medium-high heat, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the browned ribs and set them aside. Add the onion to the pot and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, oregano, cumin, and coriander to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the browned ribs back to the pot, along with the avocado pulp, cilantro, lime juice, salt, and pepper (if using).
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven. Braise the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender and falls off the bone.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly. Serve the stew warm, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Strain the stew through a fine-mesh sieve to remove excess fat and serve.
For a thicker stew, mash some of the avocado pulp against the side of the pot with a wooden spoon before serving.
